---
title: "How to Be Anti-Social"
description: "Nate's satirical playbook for the patterns of thought that shrink conversations and deepen isolation."
author: synesis
date: "2026-04-24"
categories: [society, social media, links]
image: cover.jpg
also-toot: true
---

![Image from [Leaflet](https://nate.leaflet.pub/3mk4xkaxobc2p).](cover.jpg){fig-align="center"}

Learn how to sabotage yourself in [how to be anti-social](https://nate.leaflet.pub/3mk4xkaxobc2p):

- **Assume bad faith.** When someone confuses or upsets you, assume they have no sane reason for it; when ambiguous, default to malicious, ignorant, or amoral.
- **Read intent through your fears.** Trust intuition and feeling, never challenge or even acknowledge the assumptions doing the interpreting.
- **Pivot when challenged.** Steer away from questions outside your wheelhouse; treat any display of not-knowing as weakness.
- **Word questions to imply you are right.** If you must ask, lead the witness.
- **Dig in.** When dissent piles up, hold firm.
- **Curate the audience.** Reframe interactions for like-minded supporters so they can rally to crush lingering threats to the narrative.
- **Ignore credentials.** Do not research the people you speak with, unless you already agree with them.
- **Withhold grace.** Especially from strangers who make mistakes.
- **Retreat into self.** When conversation breaks down, do not seek to understand those you do not already understand.
